project.name: AI Proxy Communication Literature Review
project.output: A concise literature review subsection explaining how AI can proxy, mediate, or transform communication between people.
project.audience: A four-person research team preparing a shared literature review draft
project.collaborationMode: Local-first GitHub JSON sync through OpenCollab

brief.assignment: Write the literature review subsection 'Using AI to Proxy Communication' for a broader research paper on AI-mediated collaboration.
constraints: claims in the draft must trace back to the source matrix
definitions must stay consistent across notes and draft
two writers may touch the same draft file, so boundary conflicts must be explicit

workPackages.label: Scope and definitions
workPackages.intent: Lock the research question, key terms, and communication-breakdown taxonomy.

workPackages.label: Evidence matrix
workPackages.intent: Collect sources and code evidence for proxy communication mechanisms.

workPackages.label: Synthesis
workPackages.intent: Turn the matrix into concepts, comparison tables, and mechanism claims.

workPackages.label: Draft integration
workPackages.intent: Write, align, cite, and merge the final literature review subsection.

classificationHints: Use dependency edges when one artifact must exist before another can be accurate.
Use boundary edges when two owners touch the same concept, claim, file, or citation surface.
Use sync edges when active tasks should pause for a short alignment meeting.
Do not hide writing conflicts inside prose; represent them as task interface boundaries.
